translated: I'd be happy if my voice and the song are reaching you all right now.
Truly.
Yes.
It's such a good story. Ena...
...is truly loved.
I'm so grateful for such a wonderful story.
With that said, let's talk about the song after we watch the 3DMV created for this event.
Here it is.
Breathing within a borrowed life and a body that can't become anyone.
If I dive deep inside, the me from that day I started drawing...
...is still smiling.
Looking back, it's certainly there,
the dream that had sprouted in my heart.
Crushed by the shadow of ideals,
it's no use stroking a past that eventually turned into hatred.
This small, gentle,
belief hasn't reached yet.
Even so, parched,
the pure white scenery before my eyes,
I'll try to paint more of the future with these hands.
Even if it's not beautiful,
in my own way,
just what I see,
I will keep painting this world.
And there we go... sorry, my screen just cut out.
The title of the song written for this event is being revealed for the first time.
The title is 'The Name of That Painting'.
That was the 3DMV.
Oh.
Wait, I have it here.
For a second, I thought my own notes were Chouchou-P's comments.
My mistake.
These are the notes I sent to the staff beforehand.
I'll read them.
Just listening to it makes me feel Ena's journey, and it brings me to tears.
The title contains the characters for 'painting' and 'Na' from her name,
so it really feels like a song written specifically for Ena.
Rather than just being pessimistic about regrets, frustration, and pain,
I sang this with her resolve to turn those feelings into strength and the positive outlook she gained from that resolve.
That's what the past me emailed to the staff.
As for Chouchou-P's comments, they've been introduced on the ProSeka Station and such,
and I think they're on Twitter—or X—so please check them out there.
Yes, those were the feelings I put into the song.
There are lyrics that feel like she's looking back at the past,
but it feels like the current Ena is the one doing the reflecting.
Ena definitely has regrets,
but she's a girl who understands that those regrets made her who she is today. So even for the phrases looking back,
I didn't want to sing them with total agonizing pain. I kept that in mind while recording.
Like I said earlier, she's found her resolve.
I sang with that sense of relief and the bright feeling of finally understanding, while keeping the story in mind.
But this isn't the goal; it's a new start.
I tried to capture that middle-ground feeling while singing.
So, rather than a huge explosion of emotion, it's more like quietly reciting the words within myself to spur me on.
Like I'm patting my own chest and telling myself to do my best.
I sang it with that kind of self-encouragement in mind.
I guess I really am going to cry.
I can hear the full version...
I'm the one who sang it, though.
I'm sorry.
I'm tearing up.
I just can't help it.
Both this story and the song just make me cry.
What should I do?
I really thought I wouldn't cry this time,
but here I am.
Yeah.
To those of you who were a bit jealous that I get to hear the full version...
Don't worry.
The 2DMV for this new song, 'The Name of That Painting', will be uploaded to the official ProSeka YouTube channel at 22:15.
Please check that out along with the 3DMV.
So that means you'll be able to hear the full version too.
I hope the 2D MV is as great as always.
I haven't seen the 2DMV myself yet.
It's a bit late, but regarding my thoughts on the 3DMV...
We recorded the full version originally,
and the lines are distributed, so I wondered which parts would be Ena's.
The song starts with the other three members of Niigo in order.
In the 3DMV in my head, I thought, 'Oh, it's not Ena?'
I was wondering how it would start.
I wondered if all four or five of them would just pop onto the screen.
